intro: Retiniphyllum laxiflorum, also known as Mexican buckeye or Retinophyllum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the subtropical regions of the Americas, characterized by its compound leaves and small white flowers. The health benefits of Retiniphyllum laxiflorum include its potential use in treating various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
